The JR Miyajima Ferry arriving at Miyajima guchi at night
Sliding door or shoji to my Japanese style room at Sakuraya Inn Miyajima Island
Traditional Japanese sweets Kompeito at the Sakuraya Inn Miyajima Island
Yours truly having a cup of matcha green tea in my Yukata on the tatami floor of my Japanese style room at Sakuraya Inn Miyajima Island
The still streets of Miyajima Island at night Miyajima Island
The O Torii Gate and stone lantern lit up in shadows of green and orange at midnight Miyajima Island
The O Torii Gate at night with the moon in the sky and the tide going out Miyajima Island
With the tide out I walked out to touch the sacred O Torii Gate at night Miyajima Island
Three people look up in awe at the great sight of The O Torii Gate and the reflection of it with the tide out at midnight Miyajima Islanda
The 28 metre high five storied pagoda built in 1407 lit up at night Miyajima Island
The still streets of Miyajima Island at night Miyajima Island
Small lit up shrine in a backstreet of Miyajima Island at night Miyajima Island
Miyajima Pale Ale Miyajima Island
Morning view from my window at Sakuraya Inn Miyajima Island
Water flows down a rock pool from the Mount Misen near the entrance of Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Autumn colors and a distance Vermilion bridge near the entrance of Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Billboard map showing the layout of Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Niomon Gate the gateway to Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Temizuya or place to purify your hands with water Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Dai hannyakyo Sutra touching these as you go up the stairs is believed to bring you great fortune Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Buddhist monk ringing the Belfry Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Kannon do Hall with autumn colors Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Coca Cola delivers all the way up here too Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva or the protector of children Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Tengu with wings and long nose considered to possess supernatural powers since ancient times Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Looking out from the top of Maniden Hall Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Detail of the roof tiling of Maniden Hall Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Decarotive wooden carving under the roof tiling of Maniden Hall Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Three of the seven Jizo Bosatsu images to pour water on each one helps console the souls of lost babies and children Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Hen jyokutsu Cave Buddhist icons of the eighty eight temples of the prestigious pilgrimage route on Shikoku Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Buddhist Monk statue Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Bronze Buddhist Monk figure Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Row of Bronze Buddhist Monk figures one with a red knitted hat Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Row of Bronze Buddhist Monk figures with Yen coin donations Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Close up of one of the 1000 Fudo Images in Chokugan do Hall Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Japanese Red Maple leaves Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Looking up into a misty and autumn colored Mount Misen Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Beautiful pond with silver crane next to Kannon do Hall Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Details of the roof of Maniden do Hall and autumn colors Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Jizo Bosatsu sleeping on a cow Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
The 500 disciples of Shaka Nyorai each one with a unique facial expression Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
The 500 disciples of Shaka Nyorai parents who have lost children are known to look after them and give them hats Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
The 500 disciples of Shaka Nyorai some with yellow hats Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Side view of The 500 disciples of Shaka Nyorai Daisho in Temple Miyajima Island
Close up of a Miyajima Island wild deer Miyajima Island
Baby deer with a background of autumn color leaves Miyajima Island
Maple leaf sticks on to my umbrella on this rainy day Miyajima Island
Itsukushima Shrine sits above the sand with the tide out Miyajima Island
Itsukushima Shrine is dedicated to three goddesses deities of sea traffic safety fortune and accomplishment first built in 593 Miyajima Island
Sleepy deer Miyajima Island
The O Torii Gate floats in the water and is coated with vermilion lacquer to protect it from corrosion Miyajima Island
Shell encrusted sign post with The O Torii Gate in the background Miyajima Island
The locals maintain the island immaculately even on rainy days they can be found cleaning up any unwanted deer extras Miyajima Island
Machine that churns out Momiji Manju and a pretty large rice scoop on the wall Miyajima Island
The Momiji Manju a Maple leaf shaped Pastry filled with bean paste or various other fillings and exclusive to Miyajima Island
Sign for the nightly exhibition of Taiko drums in this hotels lobby Miyajima Island
View from the JR Miyajima Ferry Miyajima Island
The JR Miyajima Ferry terminal at Miyajimaguchi Miyajima Island
Inside of the drivers cab of a original Hiroshima streetcar Miyajimaguchi Station
The classic well presented Ekiben or Bento Box that I got for the Shinkansen breaded pork cutlet style

Giant fingers hold sushi over a couple walking through the streets of Dotombori Osaka Night time
The angry chef sign above the Kushikatsu Daruma restaurant in Dotombori Osaka Night time
The fun 3 D signage of the Yaki niku restaurant in Dotombori Osaka Night time
Japanese woman serves up delicious melonpan ice cream from a van in Dotombori Osaka Night time
Melonpan bread sandwich with ice cream and mango sauce in Dotombori Osaka Night time
The second delicious melonpan ice cream van in the world in Dotombori Osaka Night time
Spiderman climbing into a clam above a Kobe Beef restaurant in Dotombori Osaka Night time
The Dotonbori Ferris Wheel opens in Jan 2018 after nine years its been out of commision Dotombori Osaka Night time
Ichiran Ramen booth where your waiter appears from under the bamboo blind if you need anything Dotombori Osaka Night time
Tsutenkaku Tower Osaka Night time

Got this view of the A bomb dome from the top floor of my hotel which was also a restaurant Hiroshima
View of the Peace Memorial Museum and park taken from Hotel Sunroutes restaurant floor Hiroshima
Details about the Praying Hands monument Hiroshima
The Praying Hands a monument to all the doctors and nurses who helped in the Atomic bombings of 1945 Hiroshima
The famous FamilyMart where would us Gaijins be without it Hiroshima
You dont see many Stussy shops around these days but they got one here Hiroshima
Detail about the surviving bank of the Atomic bombings of 1945 Hiroshima
One of the exsisting street cars or The Moving Streetcar Museum as they are known Hiroshima
This Bank of Japan branch was the only other surviving building of the Atomic bombings of 1945 apart from the A bomb dome Hiroshima
Japanese fire hydrants in a row Hiroshima
Happy Japanese people crossing the road with a old streetcar in the background Hiroshima
Japanese man in a hat rides a bike over Heiwao Bridge Hiroshima
Wall photo taken by the US military of the Aioi Bridge before it was bombed in 1945 at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Wall photo taken by the US military showing the total destruction of Hiroshima after the Atomic bomb landed in 1945 at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Video exhibit showing the re enactment of the Hiroshima Atomic bombing in 1945 at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Wall photo taken by the US military of the flattened buildings including the A bomb dome after Hiroshima was bombed in 1945 Hiroshima
Wall plaque explains the tragic events of Hiroshima Atomic Bombing in 1945 at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Scale model of Little Boy the Atomic bomb that was dropped on Hiroshima on 6 August 1945 by Enola Gay at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Scale model of Fat Man the Atomic bomb that was dropped on Nagasaki on 9th August 1945 by Bockscar during World War II at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Scale model of the A bomb Dome after the Atomic bomb was dropped on Hiroshima in 1945 at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Scale model of the A bomb Dome before the Atomic bomb was dropped on Hiroshima in 1945 at Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Inside the East Building of the Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Student workcorps unit suit worn by 12 year olds found after the Atomic Bomb Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Three year old Shinichi Tetsuya tricycle found buried by his father was found 40 years later after the Atomic Bomb Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Watch that stopped at 8.15am the time the Atomic Bomb dropped on Hiroshima Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Yoshito Matsushiges photograph taken immediatey after the Atomic Bomb dropped on Hiroshima Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Colorful yellows of a Ginko tree outside the Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum Hiroshima
Cenotaph for the Atomic Bomb Victims with the A bomb Dome in the background Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park Hiroshima
Cenotaph for the Atomic Bomb Victims with the main memorial building in the background Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park Hiroshima
The Childrens Peace Monument a monument for peace to commemorate Sadako Sasaki and all the children who died in the Atomic bombing Hiroshima
Japanese man rings the bell in The Childrens Peace Monument Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park Hiroshima
View of the A bomb Dome from across the Motoyasu River Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park Hiroshima
Close up of the dome structure of the A bomb Dome Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park Hiroshima
View of the A bomb Dome from Aioi Bridge Hiroshima
Original streetcar or The Moving Streetcar Museum 1910 Hiroshima
Mobilizing Student Memorial Tower students mobilized to labor in order to compensate for the lack of labor force during the war Hiroshima
